linkedin:
If you work in personal injury law, you already know: semi truck accident cases in Houston are among the most complex claims in the industry.

When an 80,000-pound commercial vehicle is involved, you're dealing with:

• Multiple potentially liable parties — the driver, carrier, shipper, and maintenance contractor
• FMCSA regulations that require specialized knowledge to leverage effectively
• Electronic logging device (ELD) data and black box evidence that can be lost or overwritten within days
• Insurance carriers with aggressive defense teams mobilized before the victim leaves the hospital

Victims in the Houston area — along I-10, the Sam Houston Tollway, and beyond — need experienced representation immediately. The problem is most people don't know where to turn after a catastrophic collision.

WreckMatch connects injured Texans with attorneys from our network of 800+ participating law firms who handle exactly these cases. Our matching process gets the right attorney in front of the right client, fast.

reddit_title: Semi truck accident in Houston, TX — what steps actually matter in the first 48 hours?
reddit_body:
If you've been in a semi truck accident in Houston, the most important thing to understand is that these cases move differently than standard car accident claims. Trucking companies have rapid response teams that show up to accident scenes quickly to protect their own interests — which means you need to act just as fast to preserve evidence like ELD data, driver logs, and dashcam footage before it's gone.

On the liability side, don't assume it's just the driver. Depending on the circumstances, the trucking carrier, cargo loader, truck manufacturer, or a maintenance contractor could all share responsibility. Texas follows modified comparative fault rules, so establishing who bears what percentage of fault matters a lot for your recovery.

For matching with an attorney who specifically handles FMCSA-regulated truck accident cases in Texas, the service at wreckmatch.com connects you with lawyers from a large network who take these cases seriously. It's worth at least getting a free consultation lined up as soon as possible — waiting even a few days can genuinely hurt your claim.
